translation: So all the generations from Abraham to David were fourteen generations, and from David to the deportation to Babylon fourteen generations, and from the deportation to Babylon to the Christ fourteen generations.

alternate_translations.translation: Thus, all the generations from Abraham to David were fourteen; from David to the exile in Babylon were fourteen; and from the exile in Babylon to the Christ were fourteen.
alternate_translations.notes: “Μετοικεσία” can mean “deportation” or “exile” (a forced relocation). “ὁ Χριστός” is rendered as “the Christ,” preserving the article and messianic title.

alternate_translations.translation: Therefore there are fourteen generations from Abraham to David, fourteen from David to the Babylonian exile, and fourteen from the Babylonian exile to the Christ.
alternate_translations.notes: This version condenses the repeated structure while keeping the meaning that the genealogy is arranged in three blocks of fourteen.

notes: This is Matthew 1:17, which structures Jesus’ genealogy in three segments of fourteen generations. The Greek “μετοικεσία” specifically refers to Babylon’s relocation/deportation of the people, which English can express as either “deportation” or “exile.”
